global.title: Swarm exposes two primitives: immutable content-addressed /bytes and mutable owner-signed feeds
global.content:
Swarm exposes two storage primitives that Connect composes for sync:

- `/bytes` — immutable, content-addressed. Upload data, get back a 64-char hex hash; the same data always yields the same hash and can never be mutated in place.
- Feeds — mutable pointers identified by the pair `(topic, owner address)`. Only the owner can update what the feed points to, but anyone who knows the topic and owner can read it.

Connect's write layering uses both: encrypted operation bytes are uploaded to `/bytes`, and the resulting reference is written to a feed. This lets readers look up a known topic (for example `ph:v2:doc:<documentId>`), follow the reference to `/bytes`, and fetch the latest encrypted batch.

The separation also clarifies where encryption belongs: encryption lives at the `/bytes` layer, while feeds hold plain-text references only.
global.description: /bytes uploads return a content hash and never change; feeds are (topic, owner) pointers that the owner updates and anyone can read — Connect uses bytes for data and feeds as pointers.
